revision.comment: Altered url. URLs might have been anonymized. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | #UCB_CommandLine

revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia3]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia8]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia03]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia03]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia10]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia03]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia03]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia10]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia14]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[category:1271 births]] [[category:1342 deaths]] [[category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[category:Nyoin]] [[category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[category:Japanese women poets]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}} [[category:1271 births]] [[category:1342 deaths]] [[category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[category:Nyoin]] [[category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[category:Japanese women poets]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}}

Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 {{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buqqhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}}

She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}}

{{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Nyoin]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Eifuku Mon'in}}

On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]]

{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[Japanese people|Japanese]] [[poet]] of the [[Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1271 births]] [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] [[:en:Poet|Poet]] of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[sn:Saionji Shōshi]] [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] [[:en:Poet|Poet]] of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q3049352}}

{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] [[:en:Poet|Poet]] of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] {{wikidata link|Q3049352}}

{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] [[:en:Poet|Poet]] of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] {{wikidata link|Q3049352}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[cs:Šóši Saiondži]][[de:Eifuku Mon’in]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[es:Eifuku-mon In]][[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]][[fr:Eifuku-mon In]][[ja:西園寺鏱子]][[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]][[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]][[zh:西園寺鏱子]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]][[de:Eifuku Mon’in]][[en:Saionji Shōshi]][[es:Eifuku-mon In]][[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]][[fr:Eifuku-mon In]][[ja:西園寺鏱子]][[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]][[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]][[zh:西園寺鏱子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1271|death_date={{death date|1342|6|10}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}; {{ill|writer|WD=Q36180}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Fushimi|WD=Q373949}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Akiko Nakanoin|WD=Q106634753}}|relatives={{ill|Saionji Kinhira|WD=Q7223260}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺鏱子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3049352]] Wikidata name: Saionji Shōshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Saionji Shōshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺鏱子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 27 November 1288 – 27 September 1298 | reign-type = Tenure | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Emperor Fushimi]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Emperor Go-Fushimi]] (adopted) | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Saionji Sanekane | mother = Nakanoin Akiko | birth_date = 1271 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1342|6|10|1271|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Shōshi'''|西園寺しょう子, 西園寺鏱子||extra=1271 – 10 June 1342}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Eifuku Mon'in'''|永福門院}} (also written Eifuku-mon In), was a celebrated [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] [[:en:Poet|Poet]] of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and a consort of the 92nd emperor, [[:en:Emperor Fushimi|Fushimi]].{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8rKCOSmKEMC&dq=%22Saionji+Sh%C5%8Dshi%22&pg=PA273 |title=The Clear Mirror: A Chronicle of the Japanese Court During the Kamakura Period (1185-1333) |date=1998-08-01 |publisher=Stanford University Press |isbn=978-0-8047-6388-2 |language=en}}{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=JvRSUOMYLeMC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA192 |title=The Poetry of Zen |date=2007-02-13 |publisher=Shambhala Publications |isbn=978-0-8348-2508-6 |language=en}} Her father was the Chancellor Sane-kane. She was a member of the {{Nihongo|[[Kyōgoku (poetry)|Kyōgoku school of verse]]|[[:ja:京極派|京極派]]|Kyōgoku-ha}}, and her work appears in the ''[[:en:Gyokuyō Wakashū|Gyokuyōshū]]''.p. 160, Acts of Worship, Yukio Mishima, tr. John Bester, pub. Kodansha, Tokyo, 1990. {{ISBN|0-87011-824-2}}/{{ISBN|4-7700-1507-0}}{{Cite book |last=Louis-Frédéric |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=eifuku+mon'in&pg=PA170 |title=Japan Encyclopedia |date=2002 |publisher=Harvard University Press |isbn=978-0-674-01753-5 |language=en}} She became empress in 1288, and though she had no children of her own, she adopted Go-Fushimi. In 1298, her husband abdicated the throne. On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] {{wikidata link|Q3049352}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

On 23 June 1316 (5th year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]]), she took tonsure as a Buddhist nun and was given the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shin'nyo Gen''' (真如源).{{cite web |title=永福門院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B0%B8%E7%A6%8F%E9%96%80%E9%99%A2-35985 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2019-10-13 |language=ja}} ==References== {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Reishi (1270–1307)|Princess Reishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Kinshi|Fujiwara no Kinshi]] | years=1288–1298}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Saionji Shōshi]] [[cs:Šóši Saiondži]] [[de:Eifuku Mon’in]] [[es:Eifuku-mon In]] [[fa:سایئونجی شوشی]] [[fr:Eifuku-mon In]] [[ja:西園寺鏱子]] [[sv:Eifuku Mon'in]] [[th:เอฟูกุ มงอิง]] [[zh:西園寺鏱子]] {{wikidata link|Q3049352}} [[Category:1342 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese women writers]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese writers]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:14th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
